sn47.40:2.1: The term “development” shows that this takes the basic practice further.
sn47.40:2.2: This teaching on origination and vanishing is also found in DN 22:2.12, MN 10:5.2, and SN 52.1:3.1. It is explained in the Samudayasutta at SN 47.42. | The phrase samudayadhamma (“liable to originate”) is found elsewhere as the realization of stream-entry (SN 56.11:11.2), and as a noble one’s discernment of the five aggregates (SN 22.126:2.1). Since both these speak of noble realization, it is reasonable to assume the current passage does too. The suffix -dhamma indicates that this is not merely observing rise and fall, but is understanding the nature of things as dependently originated. | Grammatically, samudayadhamma would normally be read as an adjective, but here it seems necessary to construe it as a noun (“liability to originate”), a construction also used at SN 23.21. Bodhi also construes it as a noun: “nature of origination”.
